rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendants/ Judgment Debtors Goggin & Goggin, Terrence Goggin, And Patrick Goggin To Respond To First Set Of Post-Judgment Written Discovery; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Matthew J. Cave
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF ALLAN STEWARDS' Motion For Order Compelling Defendants/Judgment Debtors Goggin & Goggin, Terrence Goggin, And Patrick Goggin To Respond To First Set Of Post-Judgment Written Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED in part and Denied in part. Parties are ordered to check in with the clerk and then meet and confer with the goal of narrowing the number relevant interrogatories/requests for production and to draft a protective order. Parties shall then notify the clerk upon completion of the meet and confer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WOOD ROBBINS, LLP VS. ROSENDO LOPEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2018, Line 5, PETITIONER WOOD ROBBINS, LLP's Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith. Petitioner Wood Robbins, LLP's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Respondent Rosendo Lopez acknowledges that he filed his request to vacate the award outside the mandatory and jurisdictional 100-day deadline of CCP 1288. The court cannot provide Mr. Lopez relief per CCP 473. The court lacks authority to vacate the award and must confirm it. (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211-1212.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Wood Robbins is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. NANCY T TRAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Stay Under C.C.P. 664.6 And Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jeffery Mukai;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Vacate Stay Under C.C.P. 664.6 And Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to vacate stay and enter judgment pursuant to stipulation is granted. No opposition filed. While Wells Fargo asks that a stay and dismissal be vacated, the court's records do not show that either a stay order was issued or a dismissal entered in this case. However, Wells Fargo has shown that it is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant Nancy Tran of $816.89, inclusive of costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS ESCOBAR VS. CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT CYPRESS PRIVATE SECURITY LP's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Cypress Private Security LP's demurrer or plea in abatement to all five causes of action in the first amended complaint is granted. Pending resolution of the coordinated putative class actions in Los Angeles Superior Court or further order of this court, this case is stayed based on the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction. The coordinated Los Angeles putative class actions, which were filed before this action, raise the same claims as this putative class action. A stay is necessary to prevent duplicative work and possible inconsistent rulings. Gregg v. Sup. Crt. (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 134 and Farmland Irrigation Co. v. Dopplmaier (1957) 48 Cal.2d 208 are distinguishable as they did not involve two California state court actions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Cypress is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TEED VS. APRIL PATTERSON DR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 31, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT APRIL PATTERSON, DR PATTY'S DENTAL BOUTIQUE's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Defendants April Patterson and Dr. Patty's Dental Boutique's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff Richard Teed fails to show that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts to warrant the imposition of specific jurisdiction over them in California. The declaration of Mr. Teed shows that Dr. Patterson came to California in 2014, generally described her practice, and explained that she could improve Mr. Teed's look. Over the next two years, Mr. Teed and his then girlfriend communicated with Dr. Patterson. The precise content of those communications is unknown. In late 2016, Mr. Teed and his then girlfriend scheduled appointments with Dr. Patterson in Florida, where the dental procedure that is the basis for Mr. Teed's claims occurred. Per Prince v. Urban (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1056 and the authorities cited therein, the foregoing is insufficient for California jurisdiction. "[T]here is a great deal of authority supporting the conclusion that solicitation alone is not sufficient to constitute purposeful availment." (Ghanem v. Kay (D DC 1984) 624 F.Supp. 23, 24.) Mr. Teed fails to present evidence that defendants aggressively solicited him such that the exercise of jurisdiction might lie. Prince notes that courts have declined jurisdiction where the doctor advertised and listed his phone number in the forum state. (Prince, 49 Cal.App.4th at 1058.) Dr. Patterson's comments at the 2014 conference are analogous to what occurred in those cases. The other contacts cited by Mr. Teed (e.g. prescribing medicine, appearing on Good Morning America, being listed in international publications) are also insufficient to create jurisdiction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
